name: Continuum Bridge

userStory.iWant: to bind Bijou components directly to causal data sources
userStory.soThat: my UI automatically reflects the state of the WARP graph and Shiplog without manual synchronization.

acceptanceCriteria: A ContinuumPort exists for subscribing to graph node changes
The bridge can emit TEA messages when causal events occur
Components can 'bind' their model fields to specific WARP hashes
Supports 'Time-Travel' viewing of the graph directly in the TUI
backwardCompatibility: Zero impact on standard Bijou apps; this is a specialized adapter package (@flyingrobots/bijou-continuum).

outScope: The Continuum kernel itself (Bijou is just the shell)

testPlan.goldenPath: Bind a Table to a WARP node representing a list of tasks. Update the node in the Continuum kernel. Assert that the Bijou table automatically renders the new task row.
edgeCases: Conflict resolution in the UI (handling causal forks)
testPlan.fuzzStress: Stream 10,000 causal events per second and measure the TUI's update/view latency.
failureCases: Inconsistent causal state in the UI (breaking determinism)

estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(E) where E is causal events
